bundles.name: Minimal essentials
bundles.description: The small set of open-source pi extensions and skills the sf-pi team runs day to day.

pi-skills.name: pi-skills
pi-skills.description: Curated skill collection for pi (brave-search, gmcli, gdcli, youtube-transcript, browser-tools, vscode, transcribe, and more).
pi-skills.rationale: Baseline skill library that unlocks search, Google Workspace, and browser-adjacent tools the rest of the ecosystem leans on.

pi-web-access.name: pi-web-access
pi-web-access.description: Web search, URL fetching, GitHub repo cloning, PDF extraction, YouTube, and local video analysis.
pi-web-access.rationale: Everyday research surface for any pi session — sf-pi expects the web_search and fetch_content tools this package provides.

pi-aliases.name: pi-aliases
pi-aliases.description: Command aliases for pi: /clear → /new, /exit → /quit, and related muscle-memory helpers.
pi-aliases.rationale: Tiny quality-of-life win that smooths the transition from Claude Code / Codex CLI to pi.

pi-interview.name: pi-interview
pi-interview.description: Interactive interview form extension for pi — provides the `interview` tool.
pi-interview.rationale: Structured multi-question prompts for requirement gathering and trade-off exploration. Pairs naturally with sf-pi scaffolding workflows.

glimpseui.name: glimpseui
glimpseui.description: Native micro-UI for scripts and agents — cross-platform WebView windows with bidirectional JSON.
glimpseui.rationale: Enables rich visual explainers, charts, and HTML previews without launching a full browser. Used by the visual-explainer skill.

pi-tool-display.name: pi-tool-display
pi-tool-display.description: Compact tool-call rendering, diff visualization, and output truncation for cleaner TUI sessions.
pi-tool-display.rationale: Keeps long bash/grep/read outputs legible in the TUI — a significant quality-of-life boost for Salesforce workflows that inspect large metadata or log files.

pi-updater.name: pi-updater
pi-updater.description: Codex-style auto-updater for pi — checks for new versions on startup and prompts to install.
pi-updater.rationale: Keeps pi itself current without manual `pi update --self` calls. Low-friction way to stay on the latest runtime sf-pi targets.
